title: Gruha Lakshmi Scheme: ₹2,000 Monthly to Women Heads of Household in Karnataka
meta_description: Get ₹2,000 monthly under Karnataka's Gruha Lakshmi Scheme. Eligibility, documents, application, payment status tracking & FAQs.

hero.one_liner: ₹2,000 monthly direct cash transfer to women heads of household in Karnataka

what_is_it: The Gruha Lakshmi Scheme is a flagship welfare program by the Karnataka government that puts cash directly into the hands of women. If you're the head of your household—whether you're single, separated, widowed, or your family depends on you—this scheme gives you ₹2,000 every month. That's ₹24,000 per year, transferred straight to your bank account.

The scheme targets women from Antyodaya (poorest), BPL (below poverty line), and APL (above poverty line) ration card households. It doesn't matter if you work as a laborer, farmer, or stay home managing the household. The only condition is that you're identified as the head of your family and meet the income criteria. Currently, 1.28 crore women across Karnataka receive this benefit.

The money comes via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), meaning there's no middleman, no delays at offices, and no chance of corruption. The amount goes directly from the government into your bank account every month. You don't have to visit any office repeatedly to get paid. Since it started, the scheme has distributed over ₹28,800 crore to struggling households.

This scheme recognizes that women often bear the weight of household management and decision-making, especially in poor families. Whether you're buying groceries, paying school fees, or managing medical emergencies, this ₹2,000 is yours to use as you see fit. It's designed to give you financial independence and reduce reliance on others in the family.

eligibility.label: State Residence
eligibility.value: Must be a permanent resident of Karnataka

eligibility.label: Ration Card
eligibility.value: Must hold Antyodaya, BPL, or APL ration card

eligibility.label: Head of Household
eligibility.value: Must be identified as the head of the family

eligibility.label: Income Limit
eligibility.value: Neither applicant nor spouse should be income tax payer or GST filer

eligibility.label: One Beneficiary Per Family
eligibility.value: Only one woman per household can receive the benefit

eligibility.label: Bank Account
eligibility.value: Must have a bank account linked with Aadhaar

extra_sections.heading: Documents You'll Need
rows: Must be linked with your mobile number for receiving OTP during registration
Antyodaya, BPL, or APL card required
Should show your name, account number, IFSC code clearly
Can be voter ID, driving license, electricity bill, or water bill
Shows spouse is not a tax payer or GST filer

extra_sections.caption: Karnataka government's scheme for women who are the head of the household. ₹2,000 transferred directly to bank account every month via DBT.

extra_sections.heading: Common Problems and How to Fix Them
content:
My Ration Card is Not Linked to Aadhaar

Ration card and Aadhaar must match for the scheme to process your application. Visit your local ration shop or food and civil supplies office.
Ask them to link your Ration Card with your Aadhaar. This is free and takes a few days.
Once linked, your application will be processed.

Aadhaar Name Doesn't Match Ration Card Name

The Gruha Lakshmi scheme is very strict about name matching. If your Aadhaar says 'Radha Kumari' but your ration card says 'Radha K,' applications will get rejected.
You need to correct one of them first. Go to an Aadhaar Enrollment Center with your ration card and get your name updated in Aadhaar.

Bank Account Not Linked with Aadhaar

Without Aadhaar-linked bank account, the scheme cannot transfer money to you. Visit your bank branch with your Aadhaar and ask them to seed your account.
This is a one-time process done in minutes, and it's necessary for all DBT schemes.

My Application is Stuck or Rejected

Usually this happens because of missing documents or mismatched information. Log into the Seva Sindhu portal using your Aadhaar, check what it says about your application, and download any rejection notice.
The notice will tell you exactly what's missing. Correct it and resubmit.

Payment Not Received for Two Months

Check the Karnataka DBT app using your Aadhaar login. If it shows your payment as 'released,' the money may take 3-5 days to reach your account.
Banks process DBT transfers in batches. If it's been 7 days and money hasn't arrived, contact your bank and ask about NEFT transfers for government schemes.

Getting 'Ineligible' Message

This usually means either: (1) You earn above the income limit, (2) You're listed as a government employee, (3) Your spouse is a tax payer, or (4) Another woman from your household already gets this benefit. Verify your household's actual income and employment.
If you believe the information is wrong, file a complaint at the food and civil supplies office.

extra_sections.heading: Payment Schedule and How to Check Status

steps.title: Download Karnataka DBT App
steps.description: Go to Google Play Store or Apple App Store and search for 'Karnataka DBT.' Download the official app by the Government of Karnataka.

steps.title: Register with Your Aadhaar
steps.description: Open the app and create an account using your 12-digit Aadhaar number. You'll receive an OTP on the Aadhaar-linked mobile number.

steps.title: Go to Payment Status
steps.description: From the home screen, tap 'Payment Status' option in the menu.

steps.title: Select Gruhalakshmi Scheme
steps.description: Find and tap on 'Gruhalakshmi' from the list of schemes. The app shows all active schemes you're enrolled in.

steps.title: View Your Payment History
steps.description: The app displays every monthly payment—the date it was released and the date it hit your account. You can scroll back to see all 27+ installments released so far.

steps.title: Check if Payment Reached Your Bank
steps.description: If the app shows 'released' but you don't see money in your account, wait 2-7 days. DBT transfers take time. If it's been a week, contact your bank with the release date shown in the app.

extra_sections.heading: Latest Payment Updates
content: As of March 2026, the 27th installment of ₹2,000 was released on March 9, 2026. Payments are typically released once every month, though the exact dates vary based on government budget cycles and administrative processes.
Payment usually arrives in your account within 3-7 days of the release date shown in the DBT app. If you're enrolled and your Aadhaar-linked bank account is active, the money will come automatically.
There's no need to visit any office or ask for payment.
The scheme has distributed over ₹28,800 crore since its launch. Payments are made through Direct Benefit Transfer, which means they're transparent, trackable, and can't be intercepted or lost.

extra_sections.heading: Who qualifies as 'head of household'?
extra_sections.content: The woman must be the head of the family as per the ration card. Only one woman per household can receive the benefit. The applicant must hold a BPL or Antyodaya ration card in Karnataka. Government employees, income tax payers, and families owning more than a specified land area are excluded.

how_to_apply.title: Visit the Seva Sindhu Portal
how_to_apply.description: Go to sevasindhuservices.karnataka.gov.in on your computer or mobile phone. This is the official government portal for all Karnataka welfare schemes.

how_to_apply.title: Register or Log In
how_to_apply.description: If you're a first-time user, click 'New Registration.' Enter your Aadhaar number and mobile number linked to your Aadhaar. You'll receive an OTP. Enter it to verify. If you've registered before, just log in with your credentials.

how_to_apply.title: Select Gruha Lakshmi Scheme
how_to_apply.description: On the dashboard, search for 'Gruha Lakshmi' in the scheme list. Click on it to open the application form.

how_to_apply.title: Fill in Your Details
how_to_apply.description: Enter your personal information: full name (as it appears in Aadhaar), ration card number, card type (Antyodaya/BPL/APL), bank account number, IFSC code, and spouse's details if married. Double-check everything—any mismatch will cause rejection.

how_to_apply.title: Upload Documents
how_to_apply.description: Upload clear photos or scans of: Aadhaar card, ration card, bank passbook, and address proof. Ensure all images are clear and readable. The portal may reject blurry documents.

how_to_apply.title: Submit and Print Acknowledgment
how_to_apply.description: Review all details once more, then click 'Submit.' The portal will give you an acknowledgment number. Print this page or save the PDF. Keep it safe—you'll need it if there are questions about your application.

how_to_apply.title: Wait for Verification
how_to_apply.description: The food and civil supplies office will verify your documents and eligibility details. This takes 10-15 days. You can track the status anytime on the Seva Sindhu portal using your acknowledgment number.

how_to_apply.title: Receive Your First Payment
how_to_apply.description: Once approved, you'll receive an SMS confirming eligibility. Your first ₹2,000 will be transferred to your bank account within 30 days. After that, payments come every month automatically.

important_alert.text: Aadhaar Seeding is Mandatory: Your bank account MUST be linked with your Aadhaar number for payments to reach you. Without this link, the DBT system cannot process your monthly transfer. Visit your bank if you haven't linked your account yet—it's free and takes just a few minutes.

important_dates.label: Benefit Duration
important_dates.value: Ongoing (as long as you meet eligibility criteria)

faqs.question: Can I apply if I'm divorced or separated?
faqs.answer: Yes. If you're the head of your household after divorce or separation, you're eligible. Make sure your ration card lists you as the head and you meet the income criteria.

faqs.question: What if my husband is alive but doesn't work?
faqs.answer: That's fine. The scheme only checks if your spouse is a government employee or income tax payer. If he's unemployed or does informal work without filing taxes, it doesn't affect your eligibility. You still need to show your combined household income is below the limit.

faqs.question: Do I need to renew my application every year?
faqs.answer: No. Once approved, the benefit continues automatically as long as your ration card remains valid and you meet the eligibility criteria. You'll keep receiving ₹2,000 every month unless there's a major change—like getting a government job or becoming a tax payer.

faqs.question: Can my daughter apply if she's also head of a household?
faqs.answer: No. The scheme gives only one benefit per household. If you're already receiving Gruha Lakshmi as the head of your household, no one else from your family can get it. Your daughter would need to establish her own separate household with her own ration card.

faqs.question: What if my Aadhaar and ration card have different names?
faqs.answer: They must match exactly for the application to go through. Go to an Aadhaar Enrollment Center with your ration card and update your Aadhaar to match. This is free and takes a few days. Then resubmit your application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to get the first payment after approval?
faqs.answer: Usually 30 days from the date your application is approved. The system adds you to the next monthly transfer batch. You'll get an SMS notification when the first ₹2,000 is released. Then check the DBT app to confirm it hit your account.

faqs.question: Can someone help me apply if I can't use a computer?
faqs.answer: Yes. Visit your nearest food and civil supplies office or ration shop. Staff there can help you fill out the application on the Seva Sindhu portal for free. Many villages also have Common Service Centers (CSCs) that help with government applications—they might charge a small fee.

faqs.question: What if I moved to another state after getting approved?
faqs.answer: The Gruha Lakshmi benefit stops if you move away from Karnataka. The scheme is specific to Karnataka residents. If you move back to Karnataka, you can reapply if you still meet the eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: How to apply for Gruha Lakshmi?
faqs.answer: Apply through the Seva Sindhu portal (sevasindhu.karnataka.gov.in) or visit the nearest Bangalore One / Karnataka One center. Documents needed: Aadhaar card, ration card (BPL/Antyodaya), bank passbook, and passport photo. The application is free. Verification takes 15-30 days after which the first payment is credited.

faqs.question: Can working women apply for Gruha Lakshmi?
faqs.answer: Yes, if they are the head of household on the ration card and meet the income criteria. However, women who are government employees or whose family pays income tax are excluded. Self-employed women, daily wage workers, and women in the unorganized sector are eligible as long as they hold BPL/Antyodaya ration cards.
